nodes.name: domain-graph.json
nodes.summary: Domain-level architecture graph for the Archie project, defining 7 domains (Annotation Authoring, Annotation Versioning, Media Pipeline, Storage and Persistence, UI Shell and Navigation, Input and Gesture, Publishing and Export) with entities, business rules, and cross-domain interactions derived from the full knowledge graph.

nodes.name: fingerprints.json
nodes.summary: Content-hash fingerprint cache for all analyzed files in the Archie project, storing per-file structural data (functions, classes, imports, exports) and content hashes to enable incremental re-analysis by detecting file changes since the last scan.

nodes.name: knowledge-graph.json
nodes.summary: Complete knowledge graph output from the understand-anything analysis of the Archie project, containing nodes (files, functions, classes, configs, documents), edges (imports, contains, calls, configures, documents), layer definitions, and an interactive tour for codebase navigation.
nodes.languageNotes: Generated graph data with 5400+ lines across three main sections: project metadata, node definitions, and edge definitions.

nodes.name: meta.json
nodes.summary: Analysis metadata recording the last analysis timestamp (2026-05-25T06:45:00Z), git commit hash, analysis tool version, and the count of analyzed files (188) for the Archie project.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Context-mode MCP tool routing rules for the Archie project, defining blocked commands (curl, WebFetch, inline HTTP), redirected tools (Bash, Read, grep) with sandbox equivalents, tool selection hierarchy, and subagent routing configuration.

nodes.name: CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Domain language reference and architectural through-line for the Archie annotation platform, defining locked non-negotiable design frames, the project vocabulary (Library, Exhibit, Object, Note, Layer, Section), UX decisions and philosophies, named cuts, ADR references, and flagged ambiguities.

nodes.name: HANDOFF.md
nodes.summary: Session handoff document chronicling Archie implementation progress across all phases, with a live summary of the current state (working Studio and Viewer apps dogfooded on Voynich and Bidar exhibits), backlog items, shipped features (deep-link arrival, EXIF display-master bake, AV annotation, CmdK linking, publish flow), bug fixes, and roadmap for the next session.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project entry-point documentation describing Archie as a multi-media exhibit annotation platform for deep-zoom images, audio, and video, with a pnpm monorepo architecture (three-layer rendering core shared by two apps), installation steps, quickstart commands, status and roadmap, and core concepts glossary.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root monorepo package configuration defining workspace-level scripts for test, typecheck, and build across all packages and apps, with shared dev dependencies on TypeScript (^5.6.3) and Vitest (^2.1.8).

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package configuration for the Studio authoring app, a Vite Svelte SPA, declaring dev/build/preview scripts and dependencies on @render/core and @render/svelte for the rendering core, and @annotorious/openseadragon for annotation tooling.

nodes.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
nodes.summary: pnpm workspace configuration defining packages/* and apps/* directories as workspace package locations for the Archie monorepo, enabling shared dependency management.

nodes.name: 00-INDEX.md
nodes.summary: Master index and coverage matrix for the entire 20-axis prior-art survey, documenting methodology, gap analysis, CLONE-NEXT recommendations, and consolidated white-space spines A (serverless/in-browser pipeline) and B (lossless non-rect geometry through WADM).
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown coverage matrix with 12 sections spanning axes 1-20, gap clusters, and late-discovery repos.

nodes.name: 01-deep-zoom-viewer.md
nodes.summary: Survey of how prior-art repos set up OpenSeadragon for serverless single-image and tiled (DZI/IIIF Image API) sources, including cozy-iiif classification, field-studio resolver patterns, and viewport-event-to-annotation-anchor sync primitives.

nodes.name: 02-annotation-tools.md
nodes.summary: Survey of annotation shape drawing and editing across prior-art repos, with focused analysis of the W3CImageFormat SvgSelector round-trip corruption (Ellipse NaN, Path Q/T drop), liiive serialize-side crosswalk, and FastSAM auto-region patterns.

nodes.name: 03-annotation-data-model.md
nodes.summary: Survey of W3C Web Annotation Data Model implementations in the corpus, covering annomea WADM spine utilities, cozy-iiif AnnotationPage collection/pagination, and selector/scope patterns for FragmentSelector and SvgSelector.

nodes.name: 04-multimedia-av.md
nodes.summary: Survey of audio and video annotation approaches, including WADM-native media fragment selectors, the only reusable AV-to-WADM library in the corpus, papadam Whisper ASR transcript pipeline, and OSD-plus-AV coexistence prototypes.

nodes.name: 05-multi-object-collections.md
nodes.summary: Survey of multi-canvas IIIF Collection traversal, cross-object exhibit composition, and collection pagination patterns from cozy-iiif, biiif, tiny-iiif, immarkus, and tropy, covering manifest aggregation and item-set membership models.

nodes.name: 06-authoring-cms.md
nodes.summary: Survey of gallery and exhibit-making authoring UIs, covering in-browser persistence patterns (OPFS, FSA), iiif-manifest-editor Vault normalized store, field-studio Svelte editor, and papadam multi-user server-backed CMS patterns.

nodes.name: 07-published-read-ui.md
nodes.summary: Survey of consumer and exhibit viewer read-side UIs, covering juncture scroll-sync visual essays, annomea read-side audits, Research-Narratives Svelte narrative view, and papadam server-rendered public exhibit read views.

nodes.name: 08-linkable-navigable.md
nodes.summary: Survey of linkable and navigable annotation patterns, covering anvil URI scheme, IIIF Content State deep-links, quire prose-to-region linking, fitBounds, shareable viewport state, and research-narrative graph-node spatial connections.

nodes.name: 09-web-publishable-serverless.md
nodes.summary: Survey of serverless web publishing approaches for IIIF exhibits, covering static-IIIF generators (biiif, tiny-iiif, canopy), juncture runtime-fetched essays, quire GH-Pages publishing, and anvil target deployment design.

nodes.name: 10-state-mutation-patterns.md
nodes.summary: Survey of state mutation, undo/redo, and CRDT collaborative editing patterns, covering liiive Yjs annotations, excalidraw inverse-entry undo, FossFLOW snapshot undo, and papadam production Yjs persistence bridge as the most complete CRDT design in the corpus.

nodes.name: 11-20-NEW-AXES.md
nodes.summary: Definition document for second-order capability axes 11-20 beyond the original feature brief, covering search and discovery, accessibility, internationalization, format interop, provenance and citation, performance, in-browser tile generation, embedding, AI-assisted authoring, and security sanitization.

nodes.name: 11-search-discovery.md
nodes.summary: Survey of full-text and visual similarity search patterns, covering field-studio IIIF Content Search API client with local full-text index, browser-visual-search FastSAM-CLIP cosine-kNN region similarity, tropy ALTO transcription ingest, and faceted browse approaches.

nodes.name: 12-accessibility.md
nodes.summary: Survey of accessibility and keyboard navigation patterns for zoomable IIIF viewers, covering OpenSeadragon built-in keyboard layer, annotorious keyboard annotate with region-role signaling, mirador ARIA patterns, and papadam high-contrast profiles with caption tracks.

nodes.name: 13-i18n-localization.md
nodes.summary: Survey of internationalization and localization patterns, covering IIIF manifesto LanguageMap parsing and language negotiation, mirador i18next UI with Choice rendering, cozy-iiif label helpers, field-studio RTL switch, and the unsolved WADM Choice multi-lang annotation body.

nodes.name: 14-import-interop.md
nodes.summary: Survey of import, format interop, and round-trip patterns, covering cozy-iiif IIIF manifest P2-to-P3 parsing, annotorious W3C selector round-trip fidelity, tropy OCR-ALTO ingest with JSON-LD round-trip and EDTF dates, and field-studio spreadsheet-EXIF ingestion approaches.

nodes.name: 15-provenance-citation.md
nodes.summary: Survey of annotation provenance, citation string generation, and versioning patterns, covering annomea WADM creator/created emission, anvil content-hash palimpsest versioning, quire CSL-JSON citation rendering, manifesto requiredStatement handling, and EDTF date format for scholarly dates.

nodes.name: 16-performance-ux.md
nodes.summary: Survey of user-experienced performance patterns for IIIF viewers, covering JS bundle budgets, mirador virtualization at scale, clover-iiif lazy loading, field-studio build-time vendor splitting, cozy-iiif resolution-aware resource model, and papadam media-streaming with sync-write batching.

nodes.name: 17-tile-generation.md
nodes.summary: Survey of in-browser tile generation and image processing pipelines, with the headline finding that no surveyed repo generates a tile pyramid in the browser — field-studio OffscreenCanvas Worker produces only single derivatives, while all tile generators (biiif, tiny-iiif, canopy) are Node or server-bound.

nodes.name: 18-embedding-ecosystem.md
nodes.summary: Survey of embedding and ecosystem integration patterns for IIIF viewers, covering clover-iiif web component embed with Content State attribute, universalviewer iframe embed with embed-script generator, annomea embed audit findings, and juncture Vue embed host patterns.

nodes.name: 19-ai-authoring.md
nodes.summary: Surveys AI-assisted authoring prior art across 7 repos, focusing on in-browser auto-region detection (FastSAM+CLIP via onnxruntime-web), OCR/HTR transcription ingest, and transcript generation. Identifies the browser-visual-search engine as the strongest PURE liftable asset and finds no repo solves mask-to-SvgSelector contour tracing, in-browser OCR/ASR generation, or auto-alt-text.

nodes.name: 20-security-sanitization.md
nodes.summary: Surveys security and content sanitization across 7 repos, covering XSS in annotation TextualBody bodies, SVG injection via SvgSelector, OAuth token handling in backend-less SPAs, and CORS for cross-origin IIIF. Recommends lifting field-studio's DOMPurify SVG-profile sanitizer and annomea's makeSanitizer closure pattern, while identifying the unaddressed gap of a combined correct-parse plus strong-sanitize SvgSelector gate.

nodes.name: _COVERAGE.md
nodes.summary: Mechanical coverage sweep matrix mapping 76 candidate repos across 20 survey axes, with status classification (CITED, ADDED, NOT-RELEVANT). Reports 43 repos cited in axis files, 3 added by the sweep (papadam, wax-tasks, beehive_poster_viewer), and 30 justified out-of-scope. Includes under-cited repos flagged for deeper survey and surprising high-signal repos initially missed.

nodes.name: _DISPATCH-CONTEXT.md
nodes.summary: Shared dispatch context for all prior-art axis surveyors, providing the goal, repo inventory with axis-relevance tags, output contract with file template, and a Wave 3 addendum listing newly cloned repos. Requires every surveyor to read this file plus _FRAMING.md before starting work, and mandates PURE vs COUPLED tagging with real file:line citations.

nodes.name: _FRAMING.md
nodes.summary: Framing brief distilling hard-won decisions from the user's own projects (anvil, annomea, field-studio), providing the WADM round-trip verdict on Ellipse/Path geometry corruption, locked annotation data model decisions, storage/serverless architecture, glossary of exact terms, lessons learned from annomea audits, and per-axis look-for hints. Every axis surveyor must read this first.

nodes.name: _GAP-ANSWERS.md
nodes.summary: Close-loop verification of 7 gap-targeting library clones against named gaps in _GAPS.md, with DONOR-CONFIRMED, PARTIAL, or still-open verdicts backed by real file:line evidence and runtime smoke-tests. Confirms svgpath as the single highest-value library closing two BLOCKERS, identifies wasm-vips as over-sold (types-only clone, no DZI support), and recommends integration order.

nodes.name: _GAPS.md
nodes.summary: Cross-axis gap consolidation master ledger with severity classification (BLOCKER/MAJOR/MINOR), donor status tracking, orphan gap hunt adjudicating 10 cross-cutting concerns, and BLOCKER shortlist. Identifies 3 genuine orphan gaps (EXIF orientation vs coordinates, schema migration, empty/error/loading states) and determines none warrants a new survey axis.

nodes.name: _PAPADAM.md
nodes.summary: Single-repo cross-axis profile of papadam, discovered late by the coverage sweep as the most feature-complete annotation platform in the tree. Maps papadam across all 20 survey axes, identifies it as the BEST donor for ASR transcript pipeline (axis 19), production Y.js CRDT (axis 10), and server-backed multi-user CMS (axis 6), while noting it is a server-dependent media platform, not a deep-zoom IIIF viewer.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Provides an overview of the @archie/studio Svelte SPA, covering its role in the archive authoring workflow, current capabilities (canvas editor, image import, A/V editor, merge review, publish), key files, run instructions, and planned features.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry point for the Archie Studio Svelte SPA, loading Google Fonts and mounting the application via the /src/main.ts script with a root #app div.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite configuration for the Archie Studio Svelte SPA, enabling the Svelte plugin for HMR and production builds via @sveltejs/vite-plugin-svelte.

nodes.name: IMPLEMENTATION-STRATEGY.md
nodes.summary: Meta-level implementation strategy detailing the phase decomposition (0-3), ordering principles (sources before projections, adopted before invented), validation-gate mechanism, mechanical task decomposition for LLM-based execution, and the deferred-work registry for the Archie annotation tool build.

nodes.name: PRE-P3-UX-AUDIT.md
nodes.summary: Pre-Phase-3 UX audit cross-checking CONTEXT.md design requirements against shipped features, identifying 6 gaps (markers, storage chooser, intra-Library linking, deep-link arrival, EXIF bake, AV hand-annotation) and classifying the narrative layout conflict.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index to the Archie design and architecture documentation, organizing pointers to CONTEXT.md, Architecture Decision Records (ADR-0001 through ADR-0004), Q-N decision records, architecture maps, per-phase plans, and spikes.

nodes.name: bundle-size.json
nodes.summary: Bundle size measurements for Archie's rendering layer, recording floor sizes for @render/core (22.8 KB min, 7.8 KB gz), @render/core with fflate (35.1 KB min, 13.8 KB gz), and OSD + Annotorious + plugin-tools (790.5 KB min, 222.7 KB gz), with a 240 KB budget reference.

nodes.name: 0001-objects-are-exhibit-nested.md
nodes.summary: Documents the decision that media objects and their annotations are owned by the Exhibit (nested on disk) rather than a shared project pool, ensuring Exhibits are self-contained and independently portable at the cost of cross-Exhibit reuse.

nodes.name: 0002-rendering-and-framework.md
nodes.summary: Establishes a three-layer headless rendering core (@render/core, @render/mount, @render/svelte) with Svelte as the framework for both Studio and Viewer surfaces, rejecting a two-framework approach and keeping adapters thin by concentrating shared logic in the framework-neutral core.

nodes.name: 0003-annotation-spine-append-only-version-dag.md
nodes.summary: Defines the annotation data model as an append-only log with version-parent DAG enabling three-way merge semantics without git, with WADM-compatible heads/history serialization; includes a May 2026 refinement separating RevId ULIDs from citation IRIs to resolve concurrency collision issues discovered during P0-4 implementation.

nodes.name: 0004-no-wasm-vips-tiling.md
nodes.summary: Rejects wasm-vips for deep-zoom tiling due to missing dzsave binding and 13-20 MB bundle size, choosing single responsive JPEG in v1 with OffscreenCanvas DZI slicing for v1.1 and external IIIF as the institutional-scale escape hatch.

nodes.name: 0005-narrative-section-model.md
nodes.summary: Defines the narrative Section as a self-contained 'third layer' carrying its own objectId, media-fragment start, and prose independent of the annotation layer, rejecting the 'tour of Notes' model so that narrative composition and annotation layers remain independently evolvable.

nodes.name: _meta.json
nodes.summary: Metadata index for the architecture documentation suite, tracking zoom-level completion status, staleness hashes for all docs, analysis sources, and the six identified subsystems.

nodes.name: domain.md
nodes.summary: Domain analysis at zoom level 1 describing the business problem, ubiquitous language (Library, Exhibit, Object, Note, Layer, Tag, Section), entity relationships, locked non-negotiable frames, and the architectural through-line with source/projection boundaries.

nodes.name: ecosystem.md

nodes.languageNotes: Two-pass pipeline (snarkdown then DOMPurify) ensures markdown display can never inject scripts; plain-text stripping for contexts where block markdown cannot render.

nodes.name: sanitize.test.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive tests for sanitizeHtml, renderMarkdown, and stripMarkdown covering XSS prevention (scripts, event handlers, javascript URLs), safe HTML preservation, and edge cases.

nodes.name: bundle-size.mjs
nodes.summary: Utility script that measures minified and gzipped bundle sizes for Archie packages using esbuild, used to track the 240 KB renderer bundle budget.

nodes.name: import-bidar.mjs
nodes.summary: One-time data import script that fetches the Bidar annotated map from COMPOST, converts circle annotations to xywh rectangles, and generates a TypeScript data module for both viewer and studio apps.

nodes.name: import-voynich.mjs
nodes.summary: One-time data import script that converts the anvil Voynich manuscript fixture annotations (SvgSelector polygons and FragmentSelectors) into Archie's data format and copies images into both apps.

nodes.name: createCanvasController
nodes.summary: Factory function creating a CanvasController that inverts selection binding between MountSurface and subscribers, with programmatic select, user selection notification via onSelect, and destroy lifecycle.

nodes.name: mockSurface
nodes.summary: Creates a mock MountSurface with vi.fn spies for setSelected, fitBounds, destroy, and an emit-based onSelect for simulating user selections in tests.

nodes.name: sanitizeHtml
nodes.summary: Sanitizes an HTML string using DOMPurify with the html profile, stripping scripts, event-handler attributes, and javascript URLs while preserving safe formatting.

nodes.name: renderMarkdown
nodes.summary: Renders markdown to safe HTML via snarkdown then sanitizeHtml, ensuring user-authored annotation bodies can never inject script on display.

nodes.name: stripMarkdown
nodes.summary: Strips markdown formatting to plain text by removing images, converting links to their text, and stripping emphasis/heading/code markers for use in list snippets.

nodes.name: jpegDims
nodes.summary: Parses JPEG SOF markers from a raw buffer to extract image width and height, skipping non-dimension segment markers (DHT, JPG, DAC).

nodes.name: jpegDims
nodes.summary: Parses JPEG SOF markers from a raw buffer to extract image width and height, handling segment skipping for non-dimension markers.

nodes.name: selectorToRect
nodes.summary: Converts W3C annotation selectors (SvgSelector polygon and FragmentSelector xywh) to a bounding rect [x, y, w, h] for Archie's data model.

nodes.name: presentation.ts
nodes.summary: Minimal IIIF Presentation 3 types: IIIFManifest, IIIFCanvas, IIIFCollection, IIIFRange, IIIFAnnotationPage, IIIFContentResource, and LangMap. Exposes IIIF_PRESENTATION_CONTEXT constant and langMap helper. Used by manifest.ts for IIIF output.

nodes.name: resolve.ts
nodes.summary: IIIF tile source resolution (ADR-0004 / Q-4). Classifies an Object's image source into a tile-source descriptor: plain image URL (kind:image) or external IIIF info.json (kind:iiif). Exports resolveTileSource and isIiifImageInfo.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand Archie's purpose: a static-publishable, multi-media exhibit annotation platform for deep-zoom images, audio, and video. The README introduces the monorepo architecture -- a three-layer rendering core (headless TypeScript, vanilla DOM, Svelte) shared by two applications: the Studio (authoring SPA) and the Viewer (Astro static reader). This document provides the high-level map you will fill in over the next 9 stops.

tour.title: Architecture Foundation
tour.description: CONTEXT.md establishes the project's locked design frames: W3C Web Annotation Data Model for annotation data, IIIF Presentation 3 for publishing, and an append-only annotation log with a version DAG. The IMPLEMENTATION-STRATEGY.md complements this with a phase decomposition (0-3) and ordering principles -- sources before projections, adopted before invented. Together they form the blueprint every architectural decision traces back to.

tour.title: Monorepo Map and Core Barrel
tour.description: The pnpm workspace is split into packages/ (render-core, render-mount, render-svelte) and apps/ (studio, viewer). The render-core barrel file has the highest fan-out in the entire codebase (34 re-exports), making it the single entry point through which every module is discovered. Reading this barrel gives you the complete vocabulary of the project: WADM types, annotation spine, filesystem seams, AV support, linking, query filtering, and publishing. This is the map you will navigate by for the rest of the tour.

tour.title: Foundational Types and Storage Seam
tour.description: The WADM brand module defines the project's identity system with branded ULID types (LogicalId, RevId, VersionId, ClientId). These branded IDs prevent type confusion across the boundary between the annotation log and external identifiers. The filesystem seam defines the core Filesystem, FsDirectory, FsFile, and FsWritable contracts -- an abstraction layer that multiple backends implement. Both files have the highest fan-in in the codebase (6 dependencies each), confirming they are the load-bearing foundations everything else builds on.

tour.title: Annotation Versioning Spine
tour.description: The spine modules implement Archie's core innovation: an append-only annotation log with a version DAG. The three-way merge algorithm walks parent relationships to find a merge base, classifies conflicts, and constructs multi-parent merge nodes. The serializer produces WADM-compliant history pages with provenance link-outs and per-logicalId version chains, including citation collision disambiguation. This design (ADR-0003 / Q-3) gives the project undo/redo at the data model level without external CRDT infrastructure.

tour.title: Core Features: AV, Linking, and Query
tour.description: Building on the foundational types and spine, the feature modules handle three key capabilities. The AV module uses W3C MediaFragment selectors (t= time ranges) to anchor annotations to specific moments in audio and video. The linking module builds a Library-wide note index and resolves intra-Library structured references to published display URLs. The query module layers tag and layer filtering over the annotation record set, extracting metadata from W3C Annotation bodies. Together they show how the core data model supports multimedia, cross-referencing, and discovery.

tour.title: DOM Mounting Layer
tour.description: The render-mount package bridges the headless core to the browser DOM. The MountSurface interface defines an imperative contract for annotation rendering, selection, drawing control, lifecycle, and event subscriptions. The createMount factory wires OpenSeadragon (deep-zoom viewer) with Annotorious (annotation overlay) into this surface. This is where the abstract WADM data model becomes visible, interactive geometry on a zoomable canvas.

tour.title: Svelte Integration Layer
tour.description: The render-svelte package wraps the imperative MountSurface in a reactive Svelte binding. The CanvasController is a headless TypeScript controller that inverts selection binding: rather than Svelte pushing state changes to the DOM, the controller listens to MountSurface events and exposes a reactive store that Svelte components subscribe to. The sanitization utilities (sanitizeHtml, renderMarkdown, stripMarkdown) handle safe rendering of user-authored annotation bodies, guarding against XSS through annotation content.

tour.title: Studio Authoring Application
tour.description: The Studio is a Vite-powered Svelte SPA that authors create exhibits in. The main.ts entry point imports Annotorious CSS and mounts the Svelte App component. The store module manages the OPFS (Origin Private File System) working store, providing read/write access to the authored exhibit library and its asset files entirely in-browser. The binding module handles browser glue: folder picker capability detection, ZIP download for export, and localStorage-based session persistence for recent libraries. This is where all the core packages (render-core, render-mount, render-svelte) come together into a working authoring tool.

tour.title: Viewer and Publishing Pipeline
tour.description: The Viewer is an Astro static site that reads published exhibits. The published.ts loader fetches IIIF manifests and per-canvas annotation pages from the static published tree over HTTP, reconstructing the same data model the Studio writes. The publish/site.ts module assembles the full published-site data tree: collection manifests, exhibit manifests, and annotation history pages. Together they complete the authoring-to-publishing loop: exhibits are authored in the Studio, published as static IIIF-compliant files, and served to readers through the Viewer with no server runtime required.
